comment: E23 distribution channel example (docs/mcp-bundle-distribution.md). A named, ordered publish history for signed profile bundles, owned by one issuer key and distributed as a plain file over any transport. The sha256 values are PLACEHOLDERS (repeated single hex digits, clearly fake) and the signature value is base64 of 64 zero bytes, clearly fake -- E23 is design-only and nothing verifies it. The history illustrates a rollback-by-superseding-record: team-v2 was revoked, so revision 3 re-publishes the team-v1 sha as a NEW active record; history is append-only and never rewritten.
name: stable

owner.comment: Trust decisions use key_id plus the member's local trusted-keys file only; display is for messages and grants nothing.

history.comment: team-v1: the original stable publish (E19 ceremony output).

team-v2: withdrawn via 'mcp trust revoke --bundle-sha256 ...'. This status is informational routing state; the authoritative revocation is the E15 CRL, which member-side verification enforces regardless (-32017 bundle_revoked).
Rollback by superseding record: re-publishes the team-v1 sha as the single active record at a higher document revision.

signature.comment: PLACEHOLDER signature: base64 of 64 zero bytes -- obviously fake, for shape illustration only. Optional in the format (MIT core); REQUIRED for registered/business-tier distribution. A real value is the detached Ed25519 signature over the canonical JSON (UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace) of this document with the 'signature' member removed.
